Source_Species.reason: NbUGT94E7 is an endogenous UGT from N. benthamiana; Table 1 and Results discuss N. benthamiana UGTs.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species throughout is Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species.reason: Mutant and infiltration experiments were performed in N. benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied host is Nicotiana benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper directly links NbUGT94E7 to early pathway derivative compounds, not to strictosidine itself.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The affected compounds are glycosylated early iridoid/geraniol derivatives such as trihexosyl geranic acid and acetyl dihexosyl geraniol.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper describes these as derivatized, dead-end/off-target products reducing purity/yield in general, but does not explicitly assign this exact functional wording to the specific NbUGT94E7-derived products.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Metabolites were measured from infiltrated leaf tissue.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results/Fig. 3 identify NbUGT94E7 as a Group A UGT; UGTs are Family 1 glycosyltransferases.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: NbUGT94E7 was among targets selected from transcriptomic data; the paper states pEPQDKN0780 targeted UGTs with high normalized counts following nepetalactol-pathway expression, and Table 1 includes 94E7 in that construct.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper states endogenous UGT expression changed in response to agroinfiltration and pathway expression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results explicitly state agroinfiltration and expression of geraniol/nepetalactol pathways altered endogenous UGT expression.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: This matches the workflow in Results: transcriptomics/phylogeny-based target selection, Cas9 mutagenesis, and UHPLC/MS profiling in infiltrated mutant lines.

Omics_Data.reason: Data availability gives transcriptome data under SRA project PRJNA841421.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state NbUGT94E7 mutants did not accumulate trihexosyl geranic acid; acetyl dihexosyl geraniol was also eliminated in nepetalactol-pathway samples; later they report no change in strictosidine yield in these edited lines.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported by Results/Discussion: candidate identification, loss of a specific derivative peak, and no strictosidine yield improvement.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion presents these UGT edits as proof-of-concept host chassis improvement to reduce derivatization.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Edited lines were reported as morphologically normal with no obvious changes in growth/development.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly states NbUGT94E7 lacks the GSS motif proposed to define the loop structure of monoglucosyltransferases.

Source_Species.reason: NbUGT85A73 is an endogenous N. benthamiana UGT.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species.reason: Mutagenesis and expression assays were done in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The gene is linked to early iridoid/geraniol derivative compounds rather than final strictosidine.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The affected metabolites are glycosylated monoterpenoid/iridoid derivatives listed in Fig. 3.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper generally frames such derivatives as unwanted/off-target, but this exact phrasing for NbUGT85A73 products is inferential rather than explicit.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Assays were performed on infiltrated leaf tissue.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results/Fig. 3 identify NbUGT85A73 as a Group G UGT; UGTs belong to Family 1 glycosyltransferases.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper explicitly notes NbUGT85A73 had previously been reported as active on geraniol.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It is endogenous in the leaf/pathway context, but the paper does not give a specific expression pattern for NbUGT85A73 itself in applied plants beyond target selection and prior activity knowledge.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The experiments involved agroinfiltration and heterologous pathway expression in leaves.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results describe Cas9 mutagenesis and UHPLC/MS profiling in lines expressing geraniol or nepetalactol pathway modules.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: NbUGT72AY1, NbUGT85A74, and NbUGT86A104 were co-mutated in some lines, but the paper does not present them as physical or functional interacting proteins of NbUGT85A73.

Omics_Data.reason: SRA PRJNA841421 is listed in Data availability.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state lines with NbUGT85A73 mutations in combination with certain other UGT mutations accumulated less or no listed derivative peaks, only in nepetalactol-pathway samples, and later strictosidine yield was unchanged in edited lines.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The summary matches the reported evidence, with the caveat that the phenotype was observed in lines carrying combinations including NbUGT85A73 rather than a clean single-gene attribution.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion supports host-editing to reduce background derivatization.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Edited lines showed no obvious growth or developmental defects.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results/Discussion explicitly note the effect was seen in nepetalactol-pathway expression context and that NbUGT85A73 had previously been reported active on geraniol.

corrections.reason: NbUGT72AY1, NbUGT85A74, and NbUGT86A104 were co-mutated in some lines, but the paper does not present them as physical or functional interacting proteins of NbUGT85A73.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Nepeta mussinii is explicitly stated, with note a.k.a. Nepeta racemosa.

Applied_Species.reason: The enzyme was heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: MLPL inclusion is directly tied to strictosidine production in the full pathway.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Strictosidine is described as a monoterpene indole alkaloid intermediate.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states strictosidine is a key intermediate from which all MIAs derive and discusses medicinal relevance of MIAs.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: MLPL was used in reconstitution of the strictosidine/secoiridoid-iridoid pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper places MLPL at the cyclization stage leading to nepetalactol/7-DLA production.

Enzyme_Name.reason: MLPL is expanded as major latex protein-like enzyme; Fig. 4/Results use MLPL.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is explicitly called a major latex protein-like enzyme.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results state MLPL assists with cyclization of the reactive enol product of ISY.

Primary_Product.reason: Inclusion of MLPL enabled production of authentic 7-DLA and improved flux through nepetalactol-forming chemistry.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states MLPL assists/couples cyclization of the reactive enol from ISY and controls stereochemistry relevant to the strictosidine pathway.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: MLPL enhances precursor formation upstream of strictosidine.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly presents MLPL as critical for improving flux and overcoming a bottleneck.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: This is a mid-pathway iridoid cyclization step.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was heterologously expressed by transient infiltration in leaves.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state MoClo constructs used CaMV35S promoter and TMV 5'UTR, but the paper does not explicitly map MLPL1 specifically to that promoter in Results.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The enzyme was tested by transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results show omission/addition tests with metabolite profiling against 7-DLA and strictosidine standards.

Omics_Data.reason: PRJNA841421 is listed, though not specific to MLPL.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper says ISY works in combination with MLPL and MLPL assists cyclization of the ISY product, but it does not demonstrate direct physical interaction with CrISY.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Products were quantified from leaf tissue.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported: 7-DLA enabled, strictosidine reconstruction enabled, and >60-fold strictosidine increase.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper shows MLPL strongly promotes iridoid flux and is essential for efficient strictosidine accumulation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion frames MLPL as critical/high-value for heterologous strictosidine engineering.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: No growth penalty or specific tradeoff is reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The title of the Results section and abstract explicitly emphasize MLPL as essential/critical for flux and 7-DLA production.
